labels.notes: Carter Young-watling 2:19pm — adolescent with acne vulgaris, prescribed triple-ingredient cream. SOAP correct. Billing A007A correct. DIAGNOSTIC CODE 701 (Hyperkeratosis, scleroderma, keloid) WRONG — primary diagnosis is acne vulgaris which maps to 706 (Acne, acne vulgaris, sebaceous cyst). Worse: tools-model emitted reasoning 'Code 701 is the specific OHIP diagnostic code for acne' which is internally contradictory with the DB description (701 is hyperkeratosis/scleroderma/keloid). This is a pure tools-model hallucination that bypassed validation because the code is in-DB. Needs DB-description-vs-primary-diagnosis cross-check before accepting tools-model output.
